author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
su-ramyādi guṇa-gaṇa, hoiyāche vibhūṣaṇa,
lalanā⋅ucita jatadura
pṛthupremā, su-mādhurya⋅, sampadera suprācurya,
śrī⋅kṛṣṇavallabhā rasapura

vallabhā ta’ dvi-prakāra, svīyā, parakīyā āra,
mugdhā, madhyā, pragalbheti traya
keha vā nāyikā tāhe, keha sakhī hoite cāhe,
nije ta’ nāyikā nāhi haya

nāyikāgaṇa⋅pradhāna, rādhā, candrā⋅dui jana,
saundarya⋅vaidagdhya⋅guṇāśrayā
sei dui madhye śreṣṭha, rādhikā kṛṣṇera preṣṭha,
mahābhāvasvarūpa⋅nilayā

āra jata nitya-priyā, nija nija jūtha lañā,
se du’yera karena sevana
śrī⋅rūpa⋅anuga jana, śrī⋅rādhikā⋅śrī⋅caraṇa,
vinā nāhi jāne anya dhana
en_translation:
All charming qualities and more have become her adornments, as much as befits a beautiful woman. She possesses abundant love, great sweetness, and profuse opulence. She is Śrī Kṛṣṇa's beloved, brimming with divine mellows.

Beloveds are of two kinds: one's own wife and another's wife, and these are categorized into three: innocent, partially experienced, and fully experienced. Some among them are heroines, and some wish to be companions, but she herself is not merely a heroine.

The chief among the heroines are Rādhā and Candrā, both being the abode of beauty, intelligence, and all good qualities. Among these two, Rādhikā is the greatest, Kṛṣṇa's dearest, and the ultimate abode of the highest ecstatic love.

And all other eternal beloveds, taking their respective groups, serve those two. The followers of Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī know no other wealth except the auspicious lotus feet of Śrī Rādhikā.
